title_id: Bagian 16 — Surat kepada Raja Siprus: Kemenangan Islam dan Pertanyaan tentang Tawanan Muslim
content_id:
Musuh-musuh mereka terlampaui, dikalahkan, dan dibuat tercengang. Dengan demikian, Allah telah menyempurnakan karunia-Nya dan menolong hamba-hamba-Nya sehingga meninggikan kemuliaan Islam, sebuah agama yang menanjak, meninggalkan musuh-musuh mereka dalam kekacauan dan kepedihan. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Allah akan mengutus kepada umat ini pada awal setiap abad orang yang akan memperbarui agamanya."

Raja, sebagaimana aku yakin, sangat akrab dengan kisah delegasi orang-orang Kristen Najran yang datang kepada Nabi Muhammad ﷺ. Nabi mengajak mereka kepada Islam, dan mereka bertanya kepadanya tentang al-Masih. Mereka berdebat dengannya, dan ketika argumen-argumen mereka dikalahkan dengan baik, mereka mengambil cara-cara yang licin dan taktik-taktik yang menipu, hingga Nabi diperintahkan untuk mengajak mereka kepada Mubahalah: "Siapa yang membantahmu tentang kisah 'Isa sesudah datang ilmu (yang meyakinkan kamu), maka katakanlah (kepadanya): 'Marilah kita memanggil anak-anak kami dan anak-anak kamu, isteri-isteri kami dan isteri-isteri kamu, diri kami dan diri kamu; kemudian marilah kita bermubahalah kepada Allah dan kita minta supaya laknat Allah ditimpakan kepada orang-orang yang berdusta.'" (3:61). Delegasi itu berunding dan mencapai kesepakatan berdasarkan rasa takut mereka akan konsekuensinya dan berkata: "Kamu sepenuhnya tahu bahwa ia adalah seorang Nabi, dan tidak ada yang pernah berhasil ketika mengambil tantangan mubahalah seorang nabi." Maka mereka membayar jizyah.

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ juga mengirimkan surat kepada Kaisar, raja orang-orang Kristen dari Syria hingga Konstantinopel. Raja yang mulia ini mengetahui ketika membaca surat tersebut tentang keaslian kenabian Muhammad ﷺ sebagaimana yang dinubuatkan oleh al-Masih dan yang dijanjikan oleh Ibrahim kepada Ismail. Kaisar kemudian mendorong rakyatnya untuk menghormati duta Nabi dan ia memegang surat tersebut, menciumnya, dan menempatkannya di matanya, seraya berkata: "Seandainya bukan karena kerajaan dan pemerintahan ini, aku akan senang pergi kepadanya dan membasuh kakinya."

Aku bertanya kepadamu, wahai Raja! Apakah al-Masih alaihissalam, atau para muridnya setelahnya, menyerukan perjuangan melawan umat kami? Bagaimana kalian bisa menghalalkan penumpahan darah, perampasan perempuan dan penyitaan tanah tanpa alasan apapun? Apakah ia tidak tahu bahwa kami memiliki sejumlah besar orang-orang yang dilindungi oleh perjanjian dzimmah yang tinggal di tanah-tanah kami, di bawah otoritas kami, terlindungi dan aman? Bagaimana kemudian saudara-saudara Muslim kami diperlakukan dengan cara yang tidak dapat diterima oleh orang-orang yang beragama dan terhormat? Bagaimana pengkhianatan yang licik ketika para tawanan kami ditangkap dapat dibenarkan, dan pengkhianatan dilarang dalam semua agama, aliran, dan cara-cara yang bijaksana? Bagaimana kalian bisa menghalalkan bagi diri kalian sendiri tindakan penipuan? Apakah kalian tidak takut bahwa umat Muslim akan membalas kalian dengan cara yang serupa, dan Allah akan menjadi penolong dan pembimbing mereka?
content_en: Their enemies were surpassed, defeated and dazzled. Thus, Allaah had completed His favour and aided His servants and thereby promoted the glory of Islaam, a religion of ascendance, leaving their enemies in disarray and anguish. The Prophet (saw) said, "Allaah will send this nation at the helm of every century he who will revive its religion". The King, as I am sure, is well acquainted with the story of the deputation of Najran's Christians who came to Prophet Muhammad (saw). The Prophet invited them to Islaam, and they asked him about the Messiah. They debated with him, and when their arguments were soundly beaten they resorted to slippery ways and deceitful tactics, until the Prophet was commanded to invite them to Mubahalah. 29: "And if anyone should argue with you about this [truth] after all the knowledge that has come unto you, say: "Come, let us summon our sons and your sons, and our women and your women, and ourselves and yourselves; and then let us pray [together] humbly and ardently, and let us invoke Allaah's curse upon those [of us] who are telling a lie". (3:61) The deputation consulted among itself reaching an agreement based on their fear of the consequences and saying, "You know fully that he is a Prophet, and none has ever succeeded when taken up a prophet's challenge of a Mubahala", and so they paid the jizyah 30 The Prophet Muhammad (saw) had sent a letter to Caesar, the king of the Christians from Syria to Constantinople. This king was a noble man and he knew when reading the letter, of the authenticity of Muhammad's (saw) prophethood as prophesied by the Messiah and promised by Ibraaheem to Isma'eel. Caesar then urged his people to honour the Prophet's ambassador and he held the letter, kissed it and put it on his eyes, saying, "If it was not for this kingdom and rule, I would have loved to go to him and wash his feet". 29 The "jizyah" is the exemption tax non-Muslims pay when living under Islaamic rule 30 These are the names of the three Angels The Ashamah Negus was another noble Christian king. He had met the migrant believers in Abyssinia and migrated himself to Allaah as a truthful believer, crying at the recitation of surah Maryam and saying, "By God, these words and those which were revealed to Moosa are rays of light which have radiated from the same source!" Ashamah Negus had also sent his son, among others of his people, to the Prophet (saw), and was prayed upon by the Prophet when he died. In the Seerah of the Prophet of Allaah Muhammad (saw), many examples are to be found of his equitable and honourable treatment towards the Christians. Whoever embraced Islaam from amongst them became one of Muhammad's (saw) nation with all subsequent obligations and responsibilities. He indeed is liable to be rewarded twice, one for his faith in 'Eesaa and the other for faith in Muhammad (saw). He who did not believe, on the other hand, was liable to be fought, as Allaah instructed, "Fight against those who - despite having been vouchsafed revelation - do not believe either in Allaah or the Last Day, and do not consider forbidden that which Allaah and His Messenger have forbidden, and do not follow the religion of truth [Which, Allaah has enjoined upon them] till they [agree to] pay the jizyah with a willing submission, and feel themselves subdued". (9:29) I ask you, Oh King! Has the Messiah, 'alayhis salaam, or indeed did the disciples after him, call for the struggle against our nation? How could you then make lawful the spilling of blood, seizure of women and confiscation of land without any justification? Does he not know that we have a great number of protected people of dhimmah resident in our lands, under our authority, protected and safe? How is it then that our Muslim brothers are treated in a way that is unacceptable to people of religion and honour? I do not, however, accuse the King, his brothers and family directly, as I know of the hospitality and good spirit that Abul-'Abbas was received with and his acknowledgement and praise of it. Yet, are not our Muslim prisoners jaild at his pleasure and under his rule? And have not the commandments of the Messiah and the rest of the prophets prohibited acts of castigation and enjoined acts of good will? Furthermore, how can the treacherous way our prisoners were captured be justified and treachery is forbidden in all religions, sects and wise ways. How can you render lawful for yourselves an act of deceit? Are you not afraid that Muslims will reward you in a similar way, and Allaah will be their helper and guide?
content_ar:
هذا الجزء من كتاب رسائل ابن تيمية من السجن يتناول موضوع: الجزء السادس عشر. يُقدّم هذا القسم مادةً علميةً غنيةً مستمدةً من المصادر الإسلامية الأصيلة، تجمع بين الدقة في الاستدلال والوضوح في العرض والشمولية في تناول المسائل.

يعرض المؤلف في هذا الباب خلاصة ما وصل إليه أهل العلم في المسألة المبحوثة، مستنداً إلى الأدلة الشرعية من القرآن الكريم والسنة النبوية وأقوال العلماء المعتمدين عبر القرون.

ومن أبرز ما يتميز به هذا الكتاب: الجمع بين الأصالة العلمية والتيسير على القارئ، بحيث تكون المعلومات في متناول المتخصص والمثقف على حدٍّ سواء. كما يُعنى المؤلف بربط العلوم النظرية بتطبيقاتها العملية في حياة المسلم.

هذا الكتاب في مجمله يُشكّل إضافة نوعية في مكتبة العلوم الإسلامية، ومرجعاً نافعاً لكل باحث في شؤون الإسلام وعلومه.
